mnemonics.title: Eucalyptus Components
mnemonics.content: C-W-C-S-N: Cloud controller, Walrus, Cluster controller, Storage controller, Node controller - Remember as 'Clear Water Cleans Sweaty Nodes'

acronyms.expansion: Elastic Utility Computing Architecture for Linking Your Programs
acronyms.usage: The full name of the open-source private cloud platform
The entry point for all cloud requests in Eucalyptus
Manages cluster resources and VM scheduling within a cluster
Runs on compute nodes, manages hypervisors and VM lifecycle
Machine image containing OS and software for VM instances
Amazon's object storage service that Walrus is compatible with

visualTips.visualization: Draw a hierarchical diagram: Top is Cloud Controller (CLC), below it Walrus and multiple Cluster Controllers (CC), each CC connects to multiple Node Controllers (NC). Storage Controller (SC) connects to CCs. All CCs and NCs at bottom connect to hypervisors.
User request → CLC (authenticates/schedules) → CC (finds suitable NC) → NC (launches VM). Storage: VM accesses Walrus for images (object) and SC for volumes (block).

analogies.analogy: Think of CLC as the main receptionist in a hotel - all guests (requests) must go through them, they manage who gets which room (VM) and keep track of all bookings (user accounts).
Walrus is like a library - it stores all the books (machine images) that guests (VMs) can borrow and read. It's the central repository for all reusable software.
CC is like a floor manager in a hotel - they manage a specific floor (cluster) and decide which room (VM) on their floor gets which guest based on room availability.
NC is like the housekeeping staff who actually prepares and maintains each room (compute node), managing the physical resources for guests (VMs).
